title: Company Secretary Services
description: Private companies aren't legally required to appoint a company secretary — but the governance obligations don't disappear because you're a (Pty) Ltd. CIPC filings have deadlines. Board resolutions need proper documentation. Statutory registers must be kept current. OptiHR handles all of it, with legal-grade precision and at a fraction of the cost of a full-time appointment.

whyOptiHR.title: CIPC filings & compliance
whyOptiHR.subtitle: deadlines met
whyOptiHR.body: Annual returns, director changes, share transfers, and MOI amendments — filed correctly and on time. Late filings carry penalties and risk deregistration.

whyOptiHR.title: Statutory records & registers
whyOptiHR.subtitle: audit-ready
whyOptiHR.body: We maintain your share register, directors' register, and beneficial ownership records — accurate, current, and ready for inspection.

whyOptiHR.title: Board resolutions & governance
whyOptiHR.subtitle: properly documented
whyOptiHR.body: Board decisions need to be recorded the right way. We draft resolutions, take minutes, and keep your governance defensible if a decision is ever challenged.

legalConsiderations.title: Companies Act
legalConsiderations.subtitle: governance obligations for private companies
legalConsiderations.body: Even (Pty) Ltd companies must comply with the Companies Act — maintaining registers, filing with CIPC, and documenting board decisions correctly.

legalConsiderations.title: CIPC
legalConsiderations.subtitle: annual returns and director updates
legalConsiderations.body: All companies must file annual returns and notify CIPC of changes to directors, registered addresses, and share structure within prescribed timeframes.

faq.question: Do private companies need a company secretary?
faq.answer: Private companies are not legally required to appoint a company secretary under the Companies Act, but they still have the same governance obligations as public companies. Someone needs to maintain statutory records, file with CIPC, and document board decisions — doing it wrong has consequences.

faq.question: What happens if we miss a CIPC filing deadline?
faq.answer: Late annual returns attract penalties that increase the longer they remain outstanding. Persistent non-compliance can result in the company being deregistered by CIPC, which can have serious legal and commercial implications.

faq.question: What is a statutory register and why does it matter?
faq.answer: Statutory registers include your share register, directors' register, and records of beneficial owners. These must be kept up to date and available for inspection. Failure to maintain them correctly can expose directors to personal liability.

faq.question: Can OptiHR handle company secretary services remotely?
faq.answer: Yes. Our company secretary services are designed to work efficiently for any private company in South Africa, regardless of location. We manage filings, records, and governance documentation on your behalf.

faqSubtitle: Common questions about company secretary services
faqBody: Answers to questions we hear from business owners and directors about CIPC compliance and governance.
